title: mentha longifolia subsp. typhoides
intro: Mentha longifolia subsp. typhoides, also known as Woolly Mint or Egyptian Peppermint, is a subspecies of the Mentha longifolia plant, a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to aid digestion and relieve nausea, as well as its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Woolly Mint include relief from respiratory issues, such as colds and coughs, and its antispasmodic properties can help alleviate menstrual cramps and other muscle spasms. Woolly Mint can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to calm digestive issues, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being.
